quizQuestion:
彼は貧しい{___}、いつも笑顔を忘れない。
(Although he is poor, he never forgets to smile.)
explanation: ながらも (although/even though) — 貧しいながらも、いつも笑顔を忘れない。Attaches to adj/verb stem to show contrast.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
申し訳ない{___}、今回はお断りさせていただきます。
(Although I am sorry, I must decline this time.)
explanation: ながらも (although) — 申し訳ないながらも、今回はお断りさせていただきます。Used to acknowledge a situation while stating a contrasting result.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
小さい{___}、この店は地域で一番人気がある。
(Although it is small, this shop is the most popular in the area.)
explanation: ながらも (although) — 小さいながらも、この店は地域で一番人気がある。Concedes a fact then presents a surprising contrast.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
不安{___}、彼女は一人で海外に旅立った。
(Although she was anxious, she set off abroad alone.)
explanation: ながらも (although/even though) — 不安ながらも、彼女は一人で海外に旅立った。Shows courage despite a negative state.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
春になった。{___}、まだ朝は寒い。
(Spring has come. That said, mornings are still cold.)
explanation: とはいえ (though/that said) — 春になった。とはいえ、まだ朝は寒い。Acknowledges a stated fact before presenting a qualification.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は天才だ。{___}、努力なしには成功できない。
(He is a genius. Even so, he cannot succeed without effort.)
explanation: とはいえ (even so/though) — 彼は天才だ。とはいえ、努力なしには成功できない。Concedes the prior claim while adding a contrasting condition.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
慣れてきた{___}、まだ難しいと感じる部分もある。
(Though I've gotten used to it, there are still parts I find difficult.)
explanation: とはいえ (though) — 慣れてきたとはいえ、まだ難しいと感じる部分もある。Concedes partial progress while noting remaining difficulty.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
試験には合格した。{___}、実力がついたとは言えない。
(I passed the exam. That said, I can't say I've truly improved.)
explanation: とはいえ (that said) — 試験には合格した。とはいえ、実力がついたとは言えない。Qualifies apparent success with honest self-assessment.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
日本語を勉強した{___}、まだ会話は難しい。
(Although I studied Japanese, conversation is still difficult.)
explanation: ものの (although) — 日本語を勉強したものの、まだ会話は難しい。Used after past tense to show the result fell short of expectation.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
契約書にサインした{___}、内容をよく読んでいなかった。
(Although I signed the contract, I hadn't read it carefully.)
explanation: ものの (although) — 契約書にサインしたものの、内容をよく読んでいなかった。Shows an action taken without full follow-through.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
買っては見た{___}、使い方がわからない。
(Although I bought it, I don't know how to use it.)
explanation: ものの (although) — 買ってはみたものの、使い方がわからない。Describes an attempt whose result was unsatisfying.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
引っ越した{___}、近所に知り合いが一人もいない。
(Although I moved, I don't know a single person in the neighbourhood.)
explanation: ものの (although) — 引っ越したものの、近所に知り合いが一人もいない。Acknowledges the move while noting an unresolved problem.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
悪いとわかり{___}、やめられなかった。
(Even though I knew it was wrong, I couldn't stop.)
explanation: つつも (even though/while) — 悪いとわかりつつも、やめられなかった。Used with verb stem to show awareness conflicting with behaviour.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
反対し{___}、最終的には賛成した。
(Even though I was against it, I ultimately agreed.)
explanation: つつも (even though) — 反対しつつも、最終的には賛成した。Verb stem + つつも expresses internal conflict.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
心配し{___}、彼女は何も言わなかった。
(Even though she was worried, she said nothing.)
explanation: つつも (even though) — 心配しつつも、彼女は何も言わなかった。Expresses suppressed feeling alongside contrary action.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
疲れを感じ{___}、彼は仕事を続けた。
(Even though he felt tired, he continued working.)
explanation: つつも (even though) — 疲れを感じつつも、彼は仕事を続けた。Perseverance despite an acknowledged state.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
大統領{___}、法律を無視することはできない。
(Even if one is the president, one cannot ignore the law.)
explanation: といえども (even if/even though — formal) — 大統領といえども、法律を無視することはできない。Used in formal/written contexts to concede high status.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
専門家{___}、すべてを知っているわけではない。
(Even if one is an expert, it doesn't mean one knows everything.)
explanation: といえども (even if) — 専門家といえども、すべてを知っているわけではない。Formal concession used to challenge assumptions about authority.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
親{___}、子供のすべてを決める権利はない。
(Even if one is a parent, one has no right to decide everything for one's child.)
explanation: といえども (even if — formal) — 親といえども、子供のすべてを決める権利はない。Formal register; expresses limits even on respected roles.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
どんな理由{___}、暴力は許されない。
(No matter what the reason, violence is not permitted.)
explanation: といえども (even if) — どんな理由といえども、暴力は許されない。Formal concession with universal application.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
お金がある{___}、何でも買っていいわけではない。
(Just because you have money doesn't mean you can buy anything.)
explanation: からといって (just because) — お金があるからといって、何でも買っていいわけではない。Rejects a false logical conclusion.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
忙しい{___}、食事を抜いてはいけない。
(Just because you are busy doesn't mean you should skip meals.)
explanation: からといって (just because) — 忙しいからといって、食事を抜いてはいけない。Cautions against using a reason as justification.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
失敗した{___}、すぐに諦めてはいけない。
(Just because you failed doesn't mean you should give up immediately.)
explanation: からといって (just because) — 失敗したからといって、すぐに諦めてはいけない。Challenges the reasoning behind a proposed action.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
頭がいい{___}、必ず成功するとは限らない。
(Just because someone is smart doesn't mean they will definitely succeed.)
explanation: からといって (just because) — 頭がいいからといって、必ず成功するとは限らない。Challenges an assumed cause-effect relationship.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
雨{___}、試合は予定通り行われた。
(Despite the rain, the match was held as planned.)
explanation: にもかかわらず (despite/in spite of) — 雨にもかかわらず、試合は予定通り行われた。Formal expression indicating a result contrary to circumstances.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
何度も注意した{___}、彼は同じミスを繰り返した。
(Despite being warned many times, he repeated the same mistake.)
explanation: にもかかわらず (despite) — 何度も注意したにもかかわらず、彼は同じミスを繰り返した。Strong concession emphasizing the unexpected outcome.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
病気{___}、彼女は出社してきた。
(Despite her illness, she came in to work.)
explanation: にもかかわらず (despite) — 病気にもかかわらず、彼女は出社してきた。Highlights determination in the face of adversity.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
努力した{___}、結果はよくなかった。
(Despite having made an effort, the result was not good.)
explanation: にもかかわらず (despite) — 努力したにもかかわらず、結果はよくなかった。Used to express disappointment at an effort that didn't pay off.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
初めての作品{___}、完成度が高い。
(Considering it is a first work, the quality is high.)
explanation: にしては (considering/for) — 初めての作品にしては、完成度が高い。Evaluates something against an expected standard.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
日本人{___}、箸の使い方がうまくないですね。
(Considering you are Japanese, you are not very good with chopsticks.)
explanation: にしては (for/considering) — 日本人にしては、箸の使い方がうまくないですね。Expresses mild surprise at a result below expectation.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
子供{___}、難しいことをよく知っている。
(For a child, he knows quite difficult things.)
explanation: にしては (for/considering) — 子供にしては、難しいことをよく知っている。Expresses surprise at ability exceeding the norm for a category.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
10年間も練習した{___}、まだ下手だ。
(Considering he has practiced for 10 years, he is still poor at it.)
explanation: にしては (considering) — 10年間も練習したにしては、まだ下手だ。Evaluates performance as below what experience would predict.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
急いでいる{___}、もう少し丁寧に話すべきだ。
(Even if you are in a hurry, you should speak a little more politely.)
explanation: にしても (even if/even so) — 急いでいるにしても、もう少し丁寧に話すべきだ。Concedes the circumstance while asserting a standard.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
理由があった{___}、遅刻は許されない。
(Even if there was a reason, being late is not excused.)
explanation: にしても (even if) — 理由があったにしても、遅刻は許されない。Even a valid reason does not excuse the behaviour.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
冗談{___}、あんな発言はひどすぎる。
(Even if it was a joke, that kind of remark is too awful.)
explanation: にしても (even if) — 冗談にしても、あんな発言はひどすぎる。Dismisses an excuse while maintaining the criticism.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
難しい{___}、諦めずに挑戦してほしい。
(Even if it is difficult, I want you to try without giving up.)
explanation: にしても (even if) — 難しいにしても、諦めずに挑戦してほしい。Used to encourage action despite acknowledged difficulty.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
わかった{___}、すぐに実行するのは難しい。
(Having said I understand, it is difficult to implement immediately.)
explanation: とはいうものの (having said that/even so) — わかったとはいうものの、すぐに実行するのは難しい。Acknowledges what was said while softening its implications.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は天才だ{___}、努力もしている。
(Having said he is a genius, he also works hard.)
explanation: とはいうものの (having said that) — 彼は天才だとはいうものの、努力もしている。Qualifies a strong claim by adding nuance.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
休みが増えた{___}、仕事量は変わらない。
(Having said that holidays increased, the workload is unchanged.)
explanation: とはいうものの (having said that) — 休みが増えたとはいうものの、仕事量は変わらない。Admits a fact while pointing to an unresolved problem.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
節約している{___}、なかなかお金がたまらない。
(Having said I am saving, I just can't seem to accumulate money.)
explanation: とはいうものの (having said that) — 節約しているとはいうものの、なかなかお金がたまらない。Honest admission that intention and result don't match.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
お忙しい{___}、わざわざお越しいただきありがとうございます。
(Although you are busy, thank you for taking the trouble to come.)
explanation: ところを (although/in spite of the fact that — polite) — お忙しいところを、わざわざお越しいただきありがとうございます。Polite acknowledgement of inconvenience.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
お休みの{___}、すみませんが少しよろしいですか。
(Although it is your day off, sorry but could I have a moment?)
explanation: ところを (although) — お休みのところを、すみませんが少しよろしいですか。Used when asking a favour of someone at an inconvenient time.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
帰ろうとした{___}、上司に呼び止められた。
(Although I was about to leave, I was stopped by my boss.)
explanation: ところを (although/just when) — 帰ろうとしたところを、上司に呼び止められた。Marks an action interrupted at a specific point.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
ご多忙の{___}、ご返信いただきありがとうございます。
(Although you are very busy, thank you for your reply.)
explanation: ところを (although — polite) — ご多忙のところを、ご返信いただきありがとうございます。Common in formal written correspondence.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
生まれ{___}の才能を持つ音楽家だ。
(He is a musician with an innate (born-with) talent.)
explanation: ながらの (while in that state/innate) — 生まれながらの才能を持つ音楽家だ。Fixed phrase 生まれながら(に) means 'from birth / innate'.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼女は涙{___}、別れの挨拶を述べた。
(She gave her farewell speech with tears in her eyes.)
explanation: ながらに (while in that state) — 彼女は涙ながらに、別れの挨拶を述べた。Indicates the emotional state during the action.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
昔{___}の風景がこの村には残っている。
(Scenery from the old days still remains in this village.)
explanation: ながらの (retaining that original state) — 昔ながらの風景がこの村には残っている。Used in the pattern 昔ながらの to mean 'traditional/as it has always been'.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
子供{___}に、大人顔負けの絵を描く。
(While still a child, he draws pictures that put adults to shame.)
explanation: ながらに (while being) — 子供ながらに、大人顔負けの絵を描く。Emphasises the surprising ability given the subject's young age.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
多少の苦労{___}、仕事自体は充実している。
(Although there may be some hardship, the work itself is fulfilling.)
explanation: こそあれ (although there may be/even if) — 多少の苦労こそあれ、仕事自体は充実している。Formal; concedes a minor negative while affirming an overall positive.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
多少の誤り{___}、この論文の価値は高い。
(Although there may be some errors, the value of this paper is high.)
explanation: こそあれ (although there may be) — 多少の誤りこそあれ、この論文の価値は高い。Formal literary concession acknowledging minor flaws.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
不満{___}、彼らはプロジェクトに全力を尽くした。
(Although there may have been dissatisfaction, they gave their all to the project.)
explanation: こそあれ (although there may be) — 不満こそあれ、彼らはプロジェクトに全力を尽くした。Concedes the existence of dissatisfaction while praising the effort.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
細かい問題点{___}、計画全体は順調だ。
(Although there may be minor issues, the plan overall is on track.)
explanation: こそあれ (although there may be) — 細かい問題点こそあれ、計画全体は順調だ。Literary/formal expression acknowledging minor issues.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
状況は悪くなり{___}、よくなることはない。
(The situation may get worse, if anything, but it will not improve.)
explanation: こそすれ (if anything ... but definitely not) — 状況は悪くなりこそすれ、よくなることはない。The first clause concedes the direction; the second flatly denies the opposite.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は感謝し{___}、文句を言うことはないだろう。
(He may be grateful, if anything, but he certainly wouldn't complain.)
explanation: こそすれ (if anything) — 彼は感謝しこそすれ、文句を言うことはないだろう。Emphatically asserts that only one direction is possible.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
努力すれば結果は向上し{___}、悪化することはない。
(If you make an effort, results will improve, if anything, not worsen.)
explanation: こそすれ (if anything) — 努力すれば結果は向上しこそすれ、悪化することはない。Used to forcefully assert a one-directional truth.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
二人の仲はよくなり{___}、悪くなることはない。
(Their relationship will improve, if anything, not deteriorate.)
explanation: こそすれ (if anything ... not) — 二人の仲はよくなりこそすれ、悪くなることはない。Paired with a negative final clause to deny the opposite possibility.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
諦めない{___}、必ず道は開ける。
(As long as you don't give up, a way will surely open.)
explanation: 限りは (as long as) — 諦めない限りは、必ず道は開ける。Sets the condition under which the result holds.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
私が生きている{___}、あなたを守ります。
(As long as I am alive, I will protect you.)
explanation: 限りは (as long as) — 私が生きている限りは、あなたを守ります。The action continues for the full duration of the condition.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
ルールに違反しない{___}、何をしても構いません。
(As long as you don't violate the rules, you may do anything.)
explanation: 限りは (as long as) — ルールに違反しない限りは、何をしても構いません。Permits broad freedom within a stated boundary.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
体が動く{___}、この仕事を続けたい。
(As long as my body can move, I want to continue this work.)
explanation: 限りは (as long as) — 体が動く限りは、この仕事を続けたい。Expresses committed intention tied to physical capability.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼が参加{___}、会議は開催される。
(Whether or not he participates, the meeting will be held.)
explanation: (よ)うが～まいが (whether or not) — 彼が参加しようがしまいが、会議は開催される。Both possibilities are dismissed as irrelevant to the outcome.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
君が{___}、私は関係ない。
(Whether you go or not, it has nothing to do with me.)
explanation: (よ)うが～まいが (whether or not) — 君が行こうが行くまいが、私は関係ない。Speaker declares complete indifference to the outcome.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

correctAnswer: 賛成しようがしまいが
quizQuestion:
みんなが{___}、この計画は進む。
(Whether everyone agrees or not, this plan will proceed.)
explanation: (よ)うが～まいが (whether or not) — みんなが賛成しようがしまいが、この計画は進む。Strong assertion that consensus is irrelevant.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
reading: さんせいしようがしまいが

quizQuestion:
彼女が{___}、パーティーは楽しくなるだろう。
(Whether she comes or not, the party will likely be fun.)
explanation: (よ)うが～まいが (whether or not) — 彼女が来ようが来まいが、パーティーは楽しくなるだろう。Outcome is asserted as independent of the person's choice.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
足を怪我して、逃げ{___}。
(I injured my leg and even if I wanted to run away, I couldn't.)
explanation: ようにも～ない (even if wanted to, can't) — 逃げようにも逃げられない / 動けない. The verb in ようにも is repeated with a potential negative, or a synonymous block is given.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼の前では、本当のことを言い{___}。
(In front of him, even if I wanted to tell the truth, I couldn't.)
explanation: ようにも～ない (even if wanted to, can't) — 言いようにも言えない. The desire is acknowledged but rendered impossible by circumstance.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
悲しくて、笑い{___}。
(I was so sad that even if I wanted to smile, I couldn't.)
explanation: ようにも～ない (even if wanted to, can't) — 笑いようにも笑えない. Physical or emotional state prevents the desired action.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
お金がなくて、借金を返し{___}。
(With no money, even if I wanted to repay the debt, I couldn't.)
explanation: ようにも～ない (even if wanted to, can't) — 返しようにも返せない. The pattern combines wish with an impossibility.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
完璧では{___}、十分な出来だと思う。
(Even if it is not perfect, I think it is a sufficient result.)
explanation: ないまでも (even if not/at least) — 完璧ではないまでも、十分な出来だと思う。Accepts less than the ideal while still being positive.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
毎日では{___}、週に一度は連絡してほしい。
(Even if not every day, I would like you to contact me at least once a week.)
explanation: ないまでも (even if not) — 毎日ではないまでも、週に一度は連絡してほしい。Sets a lower acceptable threshold than the ideal.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
賞をとら{___}、入賞ぐらいはしたい。
(Even if I don't win a prize, I at least want to place.)
explanation: ないまでも (even if not) — 賞をとらないまでも、入賞ぐらいはしたい。Expresses a realistic, scaled-down goal.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
トップには立て{___}、上位には入りたい。
(Even if I can't reach the top, I want to be in the upper rankings.)
explanation: ないまでも (even if not) — トップには立てないまでも、上位には入りたい。Acknowledges a limit while still aspiring to a worthy goal.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
もし時間を戻せる{___}、あの日に戻りたい。
(If I could turn back time, I would want to return to that day.)
explanation: ものなら (if it were possible/hypothetical) — 時間を戻せるものなら、あの日に戻りたい。Expresses an impossible or unlikely wish.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
逃げられる{___}逃げてみろ。
(If you can escape, just try to escape.)
explanation: ものなら (if you dare/if it's possible — challenge) — 逃げられるものなら逃げてみろ。Used as a dare implying the action is impossible or will have consequences.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
やり直せる{___}、もう一度挑戦したい。
(If I could start over, I would want to try once more.)
explanation: ものなら (if possible/hypothetical) — やり直せるものなら、もう一度挑戦したい。Desire attached to an unrealistic or regrettable hypothetical.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
文句を言える{___}、言ってみろ。
(If you can complain, just try saying it.)
explanation: ものなら (if you dare) — 文句を言えるものなら、言ってみろ。A challenge implying the speaker doubts the listener will dare.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
社長{___}、責任も大きくなる。
(When it comes to being president, responsibility also becomes great.)
explanation: ともなると (when it comes to/at that level) — 社長ともなると、責任も大きくなる。Used when a high-status role brings with it expected demands.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
大学受験{___}、家族全員が緊張する。
(When it comes to university entrance exams, the whole family gets nervous.)
explanation: ともなると (when it comes to that) — 大学受験ともなると、家族全員が緊張する。Highlights how a significant event affects those around it.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
親{___}、子供の将来を真剣に考えるものだ。
(When one becomes a parent, one naturally thinks seriously about a child's future.)
explanation: ともなると (when it comes to being) — 親ともなると、子供の将来を真剣に考えるものだ。Used when reaching a role entails natural responsibilities.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
プロ選手{___}、練習の質も変わってくる。
(When it comes to being a professional athlete, the quality of practice also changes.)
explanation: ともなると (when it comes to that level) — プロ選手ともなると、練習の質も変わってくる。Marks the threshold at which expectations shift.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
医者{___}、そんな無責任なことを言うとは。
(For someone who is supposed to be a doctor, saying such an irresponsible thing.)
explanation: ともあろうものが (for someone of that standing) — 医者ともあろうものが、そんな無責任なことを言うとは。Strongly criticises someone who fails to meet the standards of their position.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
教師{___}、生徒にそんな言葉を使うとは信じられない。
(I can't believe someone who is supposed to be a teacher would use such words to a student.)
explanation: ともあろうものが (for someone of that standing) — 教師ともあろうものが、生徒にそんな言葉を使うとは。Expresses shock at conduct unworthy of the stated role.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
チャンピオン{___}、一回戦で負けるとは。
(For someone who is supposed to be a champion to lose in the first round.)
explanation: ともあろうものが (for someone of that standing) — チャンピオンともあろうものが、一回戦で負けるとは。Expresses disbelief at an outcome that belies the person's status.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
議員{___}、収賄に関わるとはあきれた話だ。
(For someone who is supposed to be a legislator to be involved in bribery is astounding.)
explanation: ともあろうものが (for someone of that standing) — 議員ともあろうものが、収賄に関わるとは。Strong moral condemnation of a person in authority.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
今から急い{___}、電車には間に合わない。
(Even if I hurry now, I won't make the train.)
explanation: たところで (even if/supposing) — 今から急いだところで、電車には間に合わない。The hypothetical action is futile.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
謝っ{___}、許してもらえるとは限らない。
(Even if you apologise, it doesn't mean you will be forgiven.)
explanation: たところで (even if) — 謝ったところで、許してもらえるとは限らない。Cautions that the assumed positive effect may not follow.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
一人で考え{___}、答えは出ないだろう。
(Even if you think about it alone, you probably won't find the answer.)
explanation: たところで (even if) — 一人で考えたところで、答えは出ないだろう。Suggests the approach is insufficient for the task.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
もし成功し{___}、それだけ大きなリスクも伴う。
(Even if one were to succeed, it comes with equally great risk.)
explanation: たところで (even if/supposing) — 成功したところで、それだけ大きなリスクも伴う. Acknowledges a positive outcome while tempering enthusiasm.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
愛してい{___}、厳しいことも言う。
(It is only because I love you that I also say harsh things.)
explanation: ばこそ (only because) — 愛しているばこそ、厳しいことも言う。Emphasises that a negative-seeming action is motivated by a positive cause.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
信頼してい{___}、任せたのだ。
(It is only because I trust you that I entrusted it to you.)
explanation: ばこそ (only because) — 信頼しているばこそ、任せたのだ。Highlights the positive motive behind an act of delegation.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
努力すれ{___}、結果がついてくる。
(It is only because one makes an effort that results follow.)
explanation: ばこそ (only because) — 努力すればこそ、結果がついてくる。Asserts a direct causal connection.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
健康であれ{___}、何でもできる。
(It is only because one is healthy that one can do anything.)
explanation: ばこそ (only because) — 健康であればこそ、何でもできる。Health as the fundamental enabling condition.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
いくら頑張っても、諦めれ{___}。
(No matter how hard you try, if you give up, that's the end of it.)
explanation: ばそれまでだ (if ... then it's all over) — 諦めればそれまでだ. Warns that a single action or decision can nullify all previous effort.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
どんな計画も、実行しなけれ{___}。
(No matter how good a plan is, if it is not executed, that is the end of it.)
explanation: ばそれまでだ (if then it's over) — 実行しなければそれまでだ. Plans without action are worthless.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
準備万端でも、本番で失敗すれ{___}。
(Even if perfectly prepared, if you fail on the day, that's the end.)
explanation: ばそれまでだ (if then it's over) — 失敗すればそれまでだ. Performance at the decisive moment is what counts.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
どれだけ愛していても、相手に嫌われれ{___}。
(No matter how much you love someone, if they come to dislike you, that's the end.)
explanation: ばそれまでだ (if then it's over) — 嫌われればそれまでだ. Used to express the point of no return in a relationship.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼に秘密を話し{___}、必ず広まる。
(Once you tell him a secret, it will definitely spread.)
explanation: が最後/たが最後 (once it happens, it's over) — 話したが最後、必ず広まる. Indicates that once the action occurs, the negative consequence is inevitable.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あの店に入っ{___}、何時間も出てこられなくなる。
(Once you enter that shop, you won't be able to leave for hours.)
explanation: たが最後 (once you do, it's over) — 入ったが最後、何時間も出てこられなくなる. The action triggers an inescapable chain.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼女に頼ま{___}、断れない。
(Once asked by her, I can't refuse.)
explanation: が最後 (once it happens) — 頼まれが最後、断れない. Captures the inevitability of compliance once the trigger occurs.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あの映画を見始め{___}、最後まで見ずにはいられない。
(Once you start watching that film, you won't be able to stop until the end.)
explanation: たが最後 (once started, it's over) — 見始めたが最後、最後まで見ずにはいられない. Used for highly compelling or addictive experiences.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
家族のため{___}、どんな苦労もいとわない。
(If it is for my family, I will not mind any hardship.)
explanation: とあれば (if that is the case) — 家族のためとあれば、どんな苦労もいとわない. The condition is taken as sufficient justification for any sacrifice.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
必要{___}、すぐに駆けつけます。
(If it is necessary, I will come right away.)
explanation: とあれば (if that is the case) — 必要とあれば、すぐに駆けつけます. Expresses unconditional willingness given a specific trigger.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
君の頼み{___}、断れないよ。
(If it is your request, I can't say no.)
explanation: とあれば (if that is the case) — 君の頼みとあれば、断れないよ. Personal relationship makes refusal impossible.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
国のため{___}、命を捧げる覚悟がある。
(If it is for the country, I am prepared to give my life.)
explanation: とあれば (if that is the case) — 国のためとあれば、命を捧げる覚悟がある. Extreme commitment justified by a cause.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
明日、雨が降ら{___}から、傘を持っていこう。
(Since we can't rule out rain tomorrow, let's take an umbrella.)
explanation: ないとも限らない (can't rule out) — 雨が降らないとも限らないから、傘を持っていこう. Hedges against a possibility that cannot be excluded.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼が嘘をついてい{___}ので、注意したほうがいい。
(Since we can't rule out that he is lying, it's better to be careful.)
explanation: ないとも限らない (can't rule out) — 嘘をついていないとも限らないので、注意したほうがいい. Advises caution based on an unverifiable but possible situation.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
成功し{___}から、準備をしっかりしよう。
(Since we can't rule out success, let's prepare thoroughly.)
explanation: ないとも限らない (can't rule out) — 成功しないとも限らないから、準備をしっかりしよう. Encourages preparation by allowing for a positive outcome.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
また会え{___}ので、今は別れを惜しもう。
(Since we can't rule out seeing each other again, let's savour this parting.)
explanation: ないとも限らない (can't rule out) — また会えないとも限らないので、今は別れを惜しもう. Bittersweet acknowledgement of uncertainty.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
連絡が{___}、こちらから動くことはできない。
(Unless there is contact, we cannot take action from our side.)
explanation: ない限り (as long as ... does not / unless) — 連絡がない限り、こちらから動くことはできない. Negative condition that must be met to allow the outcome.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
奇跡が起き{___}、あのチームには勝てない。
(Unless a miracle happens, we can't beat that team.)
explanation: ない限り (unless) — 奇跡が起きない限り、あのチームには勝てない. Sets an extreme condition as the only path to the outcome.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
許可が下り{___}、工事は始められない。
(Unless permission is granted, construction cannot begin.)
explanation: ない限り (unless) — 許可が下りない限り、工事は始められない. Legal or procedural prerequisite stated as an absolute.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
自分で確認し{___}、信じることはできない。
(Unless I confirm it myself, I cannot believe it.)
explanation: ない限り (unless) — 自分で確認しない限り、信じることはできない. Personal verification as the only acceptable standard.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
言い{___}、相手を傷つけることもある。
(Depending on how you say it, you may hurt the other person.)
explanation: ようによっては (depending on how) — 言いようによっては、相手を傷つけることもある. The manner of the action determines the outcome.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
使い{___}、この道具はとても役に立つ。
(Depending on how you use it, this tool can be very useful.)
explanation: ようによっては (depending on how) — 使いようによっては、この道具はとても役に立つ. Highlights that method affects outcome.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
考え{___}、それほど難しい問題ではない。
(Depending on how you think about it, it is not such a difficult problem.)
explanation: ようによっては (depending on how) — 考えようによっては、それほど難しい問題ではない. Perspective or approach changes the apparent difficulty.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
料理の仕方{___}、同じ食材でもまったく違う味になる。
(Depending on how you cook, the same ingredients can taste completely different.)
explanation: ようによっては (depending on how) — 料理の仕方ようによっては、同じ食材でもまったく違う味になる. The method transforms the result.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
交渉の結果{___}、契約を解除することもある。
(Depending on the outcome of negotiations, we may also cancel the contract.)
explanation: いかんによっては (depending on circumstances — formal) — 交渉の結果いかんによっては、契約を解除することもある. Formal expression used in business or official contexts.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
天候の{___}、イベントを中止することがある。
(Depending on weather conditions, the event may be cancelled.)
explanation: いかんによっては (depending on) — 天候のいかんによっては、イベントを中止することがある. Standard formal disclaimer.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
成績{___}、奨学金が支給されないこともある。
(Depending on grades, the scholarship may not be awarded.)
explanation: いかんによっては (depending on) — 成績いかんによっては、奨学金が支給されないこともある. Academic condition determines financial outcome.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
対応の{___}、問題が大きくなる可能性もある。
(Depending on the response, there is also a possibility the problem will grow larger.)
explanation: いかんによっては (depending on) — 対応のいかんによっては、問題が大きくなる可能性もある. Crisis management framing.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
交渉の結果{___}、計画を変更する必要がある。
(Depending on the result of negotiations, there may be a need to revise the plan.)
explanation: いかんでは (depending on — formal/written) — 交渉の結果いかんでは、計画を変更する必要がある. Similar to いかんによっては but slightly more formal/literary.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
回答の内容{___}、法的措置を取ることも検討する。
(Depending on the content of the reply, we will also consider taking legal action.)
explanation: いかんでは (depending on — formal) — 回答の内容いかんでは、法的措置を取ることも検討する. Used in formal letters and official warnings.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
審査の結果{___}、採用が取り消される場合もある。
(Depending on the screening result, the hiring may also be rescinded.)
explanation: いかんでは (depending on — formal) — 審査の結果いかんでは、採用が取り消される場合もある. Outcome-contingent formal statement.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
今後の対応{___}、信頼を失うことになる。
(Depending on how we respond going forward, we may lose trust.)
explanation: いかんでは (depending on — formal) — 今後の対応いかんでは、信頼を失うことになる. Stakes framed through a formal conditional.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あの人に頭を下げる{___}、仕事を辞めたほうがましだ。
(Rather than bowing my head to that person, I'd rather quit my job.)
explanation: くらいなら (rather than) — あの人に頭を下げるくらいなら、仕事を辞めたほうがましだ. The speaker finds the alternative more acceptable than an undesirable action.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
嘘をつく{___}、何も言わないほうがいい。
(Rather than telling a lie, it is better to say nothing.)
explanation: くらいなら (rather than) — 嘘をつくくらいなら、何も言わないほうがいい. Chooses silence as the lesser of two evils.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
そんな仕事を続ける{___}、転職したほうが幸せだ。
(Rather than continuing such a job, changing careers would make you happier.)
explanation: くらいなら (rather than) — そんな仕事を続けるくらいなら、転職したほうが幸せだ. Recommends a drastic alternative over enduring a bad situation.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
我慢して食べる{___}、食べないほうがましだ。
(Rather than eating it while enduring it, it is better not to eat it at all.)
explanation: くらいなら (rather than) — 我慢して食べるくらいなら、食べないほうがましだ. Rejects a joyless experience in favour of abstention.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

correctAnswer: どう言おうが何を言おうが
quizQuestion:
{___}、私の気持ちは変わらない。
(No matter what you say or how you say it, my feelings won't change.)
explanation: ようが～ようが (whether ... or, no matter) — どう言おうが何を言おうが、私の気持ちは変わらない. Two parallel volitional forms dismiss all variants of the condition.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
reading: どういおうがなにをいおうが
pronunciation: dou iyou ga nani wo iyou ga

correctAnswer: 誰が来ようが何が起きようが
quizQuestion:
{___}、計画通りに進める。
(No matter who comes or what happens, we will proceed as planned.)
explanation: ようが～ようが (no matter who/what) — 誰が来ようが何が起きようが、計画通りに進める. Asserts unwavering resolve against any conceivable obstacle.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
reading: だれがこようがなにがおきようが
pronunciation: dare ga koyou ga nani ga okiyou ga

correctAnswer: 成功しようが失敗しようが
quizQuestion:
{___}、全力を尽くすことに意味がある。
(Whether you succeed or fail, there is meaning in giving your all.)
explanation: ようが～ようが (whether ... or) — 成功しようが失敗しようが、全力を尽くすことに意味がある. Outcome is irrelevant; the effort itself has value.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
reading: せいこうしようがしっぱいしようが
pronunciation: seikou shiyou ga shippai shiyou ga

correctAnswer: 雨が降ろうが風が吹こうが
quizQuestion:
{___}、毎朝ランニングを欠かさない。
(Rain or wind, I never miss my morning run.)
explanation: ようが～ようが (no matter what) — 雨が降ろうが風が吹こうが、毎朝ランニングを欠かさない. Idiomatic pair expressing resilience against weather.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
reading: あめがふろうがかぜがふこうが
pronunciation: ame ga furou ga kaze ga fukou ga

quizQuestion:
この成功は皆の努力の結果{___}。
(This success is nothing but the result of everyone's efforts.)
explanation: にほかならない (nothing but/is precisely) — この成功は皆の努力の結果にほかならない. Asserts that the stated cause or identity is the sole explanation.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼が辞めたのは、会社への不満{___}。
(The reason he quit is nothing but dissatisfaction with the company.)
explanation: にほかならない (nothing but) — 会社への不満にほかならない. Pinpoints the one true cause.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
それはあなたへの愛情の表れ{___}。
(That is nothing but an expression of love for you.)
explanation: にほかならない (nothing but) — 愛情の表れにほかならない. Identifies the deeper meaning behind an action.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼の言葉は脅迫{___}。
(His words are nothing but a threat.)
explanation: にほかならない (nothing but/is precisely) — 彼の言葉は脅迫にほかならない. Strips away ambiguity to name the true nature of something.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
指導者{___}、率先して行動すべきだ。
(Those who are leaders should take the initiative and act.)
explanation: たるもの (those who are/for someone who is) — 指導者たるもの、率先して行動すべきだ. Formal; sets the standard expected of a role.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
社会人{___}、時間を守るのは基本だ。
(For someone who is a working adult, keeping to time is fundamental.)
explanation: たるもの (for someone who is) — 社会人たるもの、時間を守るのは基本だ. Invokes the expectations attached to a social role.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
医師{___}、患者の命を最優先に考えなければならない。
(Those who are doctors must place the patient's life above all else.)
explanation: たるもの (for those who are) — 医師たるもの、患者の命を最優先に考えなければならない. Formal ethical injunction addressed to a professional role.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
親{___}、子供の手本となるべきだ。
(Those who are parents should be a model for their children.)
explanation: たるもの (for those who are) — 親たるもの、子供の手本となるべきだ. Sets an aspirational standard for the parental role.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
借金{___}、あんな高級車を買う必要はない。
(There is no need to buy such a luxury car even to the point of going into debt.)
explanation: までして (even to the point of doing) — 借金までして、あんな高級車を買う必要はない. Criticises an extreme measure taken for an unworthy goal.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
体を壊す{___}、働き続けるのはよくない。
(It is not good to keep working even to the point of ruining one's health.)
explanation: までして (even to the point of) — 体を壊すまでして、働き続けるのはよくない. Questions whether the cost is worth the effort.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
人を傷つける{___}、自分が得をしたいとは思わない。
(I don't want to profit even to the point of hurting others.)
explanation: までして (even to the point of) — 人を傷つけるまでして、自分が得をしたいとは思わない. States a moral limit the speaker won't cross.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
嘘をつく{___}、彼を守ろうとしたのだ。
(She tried to protect him even to the point of telling a lie.)
explanation: までして (even to the point of) — 嘘をつくまでして、彼を守ろうとした. The extreme measure highlights the depth of devotion.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
子供から大人{___}、この映画は人気がある。
(From children to adults, this film is popular.)
explanation: に至るまで (to the extent of/ranging all the way to) — 子供から大人に至るまで、この映画は人気がある. Indicates that the range extends even to the furthest point.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
細部{___}、彼は完璧を追求する。
(All the way to the finest details, he pursues perfection.)
explanation: に至るまで (all the way to) — 細部に至るまで、彼は完璧を追求する. Emphasises thoroughness that leaves no corner unchecked.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
日常の食事から特別なイベント{___}、この食材は使われている。
(From everyday meals all the way to special events, this ingredient is used.)
explanation: に至るまで (all the way to/ranging to) — 日常の食事から特別なイベントに至るまで、この食材は使われている. Spans the breadth of usage.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
過去から現在{___}、人類は戦争を繰り返してきた。
(From the past all the way to the present, humanity has repeated war.)
explanation: に至るまで (all the way to) — 過去から現在に至るまで、人類は戦争を繰り返してきた. Temporal range emphasising continuity.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
このような栄誉をいただけるとは、光栄{___}です。
(To receive such an honour is the height of glory.)
explanation: の極み (the height/utmost of — formal) — 光栄の極みです. Used in formal contexts to express the pinnacle of a feeling or state.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
こんな失礼なことを言われるとは、屈辱{___}だ。
(To be told such a rude thing is the height of humiliation.)
explanation: の極み (the height of) — 屈辱の極みだ. Expresses the most extreme degree of a feeling.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この景色の美しさは、感動{___}だ。
(The beauty of this scenery is the height of emotion.)
explanation: の極み (the utmost of) — 感動の極みだ. Elevates an aesthetic experience to its maximum intensity.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
こんな素晴らしい賞をいただけるとは、感謝{___}です。
(To receive such a wonderful award is the utmost of gratitude.)
explanation: の極み (utmost — formal) — 感謝の極みです. Formal expression of profound gratitude.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
若気{___}とはいえ、あの行為は反省している。
(Although it was the folly of youth, I reflect on that action.)
explanation: の至り (utmost/extreme — formal set phrase) — 若気の至り means 'youthful indiscretion / the folly of youth'. Fixed formal phrase.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
このような栄誉を賜り、光栄{___}でございます。
(To be bestowed with such an honour is the utmost of glory.)
explanation: の至り (the utmost — formal/honorific) — 光栄の至りでございます. Extremely formal; used in speeches and written commendations.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あんな失礼なことを言ってしまい、赤面{___}です。
(Having said such a rude thing, I am at the utmost of embarrassment.)
explanation: の至り (utmost) — 赤面の至りです. Formal and literary; admits profound shame.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
お褒めの言葉をいただき、恐縮{___}でございます。
(To receive such words of praise, I am at the utmost of humility.)
explanation: の至り (utmost — formal) — 恐縮の至りでございます. Used to express humble gratitude in formal or ceremonial contexts.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
嵐{___}、彼らは山頂を目指した。
(Undaunted by the storm, they aimed for the summit.)
explanation: をものともせず (undaunted by/defying) — 嵐をものともせず、彼らは山頂を目指した. Expresses heroic indifference to an obstacle.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
けが{___}、彼は試合に出場し続けた。
(Undaunted by his injury, he continued to compete.)
explanation: をものともせず (undaunted by) — けがをものともせず、彼は試合に出場し続けた. Commends determination in the face of physical adversity.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
激しい批判{___}、彼女は改革を推し進めた。
(Undaunted by fierce criticism, she pushed forward with reform.)
explanation: をものともせず (undaunted by) — 激しい批判をものともせず、彼女は改革を推し進めた. Courage in the face of social opposition.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
貧困{___}、彼は独学で医師になった。
(Undaunted by poverty, he became a doctor through self-study.)
explanation: をものともせず (defying/undaunted by) — 貧困をものともせず、彼は独学で医師になった. Triumph over socioeconomic adversity.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
東京公演{___}、全国ツアーが始まった。
(Starting with the Tokyo performance, the nationwide tour began.)
explanation: を皮切りに (starting with/kicking off with) — 東京公演を皮切りに、全国ツアーが始まった. Marks the first in a series of events.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この発見{___}、新たな研究が次々と始まった。
(Starting with this discovery, new research began one after another.)
explanation: を皮切りに (starting with) — この発見を皮切りに、新たな研究が次々と始まった. The first event triggers a cascade of related developments.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
社長のスピーチ{___}、パーティーが始まった。
(Starting with the president's speech, the party began.)
explanation: を皮切りに (starting with) — 社長のスピーチを皮切りに、パーティーが始まった. Marks the opening act that launches an event.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
一人の抗議{___}、大規模なデモへと発展した。
(Starting with one person's protest, it developed into a large-scale demonstration.)
explanation: を皮切りに (starting with) — 一人の抗議を皮切りに、大規模なデモへと発展した. A single spark igniting a movement.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
台風の影響で、イベントは中止{___}。
(Due to the typhoon, the event was forced to be cancelled.)
explanation: を余儀なくされる (be forced to/have no choice but to) — イベントは中止を余儀なくされた. Formal; describes a compelled or unavoidable action.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
経済的な理由から、彼は退学{___}。
(For financial reasons, he was forced to withdraw from school.)
explanation: を余儀なくされる (be forced to) — 退学を余儀なくされた. Passive construction showing circumstances left no choice.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
震災の影響で、多くの住民が避難{___}。
(Due to the earthquake, many residents were forced to evacuate.)
explanation: を余儀なくされる (be forced to) — 避難を余儀なくされた. Disaster context; emphasises involuntary displacement.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
会社の倒産により、従業員は解雇{___}。
(Due to the company's bankruptcy, the employees were forced to be dismissed.)
explanation: を余儀なくされる (be forced to) — 解雇を余儀なくされた. Business crisis context; circumstances dictate the outcome.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あの子供たちの笑顔を見て、感動{___}。
(Seeing those children's smiles, I cannot help but feel moved.)
explanation: を禁じ得ない (cannot help but feel — formal) — 感動を禁じ得ない. Formal expression for an emotion that cannot be suppressed.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
その悲惨な映像を見て、涙{___}。
(Seeing that tragic footage, I cannot help but shed tears.)
explanation: を禁じ得ない (cannot help but) — 涙を禁じ得ない. Formal literary statement of irresistible emotion.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼の無責任な態度に、怒り{___}。
(In the face of his irresponsible attitude, I cannot help but feel anger.)
explanation: を禁じ得ない (cannot help but feel) — 怒りを禁じ得ない. Formal admission of an emotion triggered by someone else's conduct.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
その美しい音楽に、感涙{___}。
(At that beautiful music, I cannot help but shed tears of emotion.)
explanation: を禁じ得ない (cannot help but feel) — 感涙を禁じ得ない. Formal expression for being moved to tears by beauty.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あんな無責任なことを言うなんて、失礼{___}。
(Saying such an irresponsible thing — there is a limit to rudeness.)
explanation: にもほどがある (there is a limit to) — 失礼にもほどがある. Expresses strong indignation that something has gone too far.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
毎日遅刻するなんて、いい加減さ{___}。
(Being late every day — there is a limit to carelessness.)
explanation: にもほどがある (there is a limit to) — いい加減さにもほどがある. Strong scolding; the behaviour has exceeded what is tolerable.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
そんな嘘をつくなんて、厚かましさ{___}。
(Telling such a lie — there is a limit to shamelessness.)
explanation: にもほどがある (there is a limit to) — 厚かましさにもほどがある. Blunt expression of exasperation.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あんな簡単な問題を間違えるなんて、うかつさ{___}。
(Making such a simple mistake — there is a limit to carelessness.)
explanation: にもほどがある (there is a limit to) — うかつさにもほどがある. Criticises negligence with exasperated emphasis.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この仕事ができるのは、彼女{___}。
(There is no one other than her who can do this job.)
explanation: をおいてほかにない (there is none other than) — 彼女をおいてほかにない. Asserts someone or something as uniquely irreplaceable.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
今この国を救える指導者は彼{___}。
(There is no leader other than him who can save this country now.)
explanation: をおいてほかにない (there is none other than) — 彼をおいてほかにない. Strong assertion of uniqueness in a critical moment.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この役を演じられる俳優は彼{___}。
(There is no actor other than him who can play this role.)
explanation: をおいてほかにない (none other than) — 彼をおいてほかにない. Artistic context; the person is the one perfect fit.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この問題を解決できるのは、あなた{___}。
(There is no one other than you who can solve this problem.)
explanation: をおいてほかにない (none other than) — あなたをおいてほかにない. A direct appeal to an individual's unique capability.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
一秒{___}無駄にできない。
(I cannot waste even one second.)
explanation: たりとも～ない (not even one) — 一秒たりとも無駄にできない. Expresses absolute zero tolerance for any waste, even the smallest unit.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
一円{___}払う気はない。
(I have no intention of paying even one yen.)
explanation: たりとも～ない (not even one) — 一円たりとも払う気はない. Absolute refusal quantified at the smallest possible amount.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
一人{___}欠席は許されない。
(Not even one person's absence is permitted.)
explanation: たりとも～ない (not even one) — 一人たりとも欠席は許されない. Strict rule with zero exceptions.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
一歩{___}退くつもりはない。
(I have no intention of retreating even one step.)
explanation: たりとも～ない (not even one) — 一歩たりとも退くつもりはない. Military or debate metaphor expressing absolute resolve.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あの人の態度は失礼{___}。
(That person's attitude is extremely rude.)
explanation: 極まりない/極まる (extremely/to the extreme degree) — 失礼極まりない. Formal intensifier attached to nouns or な-adjective stems.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
そのような発言は危険{___}行為だ。
(Such a statement is an extremely dangerous act.)
explanation: 極まりない (extremely) — 危険極まりない行為だ. Used in formal or written style to stress an extreme negative quality.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
このような不衛生な環境は不快{___}。
(Such an unsanitary environment is extremely unpleasant.)
explanation: 極まりない (extremely) — 不快極まりない. Expresses maximum degree of displeasure in formal style.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あの政治家の行動は不誠実{___}。
(That politician's behaviour is extremely dishonest.)
explanation: 極まりない (extremely) — 不誠実極まりない. Political critique delivered in formal register.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あなたのご支援は、ありがたい{___}。
(Your support is the utmost of gratitude.)
explanation: ことこの上ない (utmost/nothing more ... than) — ありがたいことこの上ない. Formal superlative expressing that nothing exceeds this degree.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
二人が無事に帰ってきたことは、嬉しい{___}。
(The fact that both returned safely is the utmost of happiness.)
explanation: ことこの上ない (utmost) — 嬉しいことこの上ない. Formal expression of joy at its peak.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
そのような対応は、失礼{___}。
(Such a response is the utmost of rudeness.)
explanation: ことこの上ない (utmost/supreme) — 失礼ことこの上ない. Can be used for negative extremes as well as positive.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
このような機会をいただけるとは、光栄{___}。
(To be given such an opportunity is the utmost of honour.)
explanation: ことこの上ない (utmost) — 光栄ことこの上ない. Used in formal speeches and written acknowledgements.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
こんな大きなミスをしたのだから、謝ら{___}。
(Having made such a big mistake, you cannot get away without apologising.)
explanation: ないではすまない (must inevitably/cannot get away without) — 謝らないではすまない. Social obligation; failure to act will be socially unacceptable.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
これだけ迷惑をかけておいて、賠償し{___}。
(After causing this much trouble, you cannot get away without compensating.)
explanation: ないではすまない (cannot get away without) — 賠償しないではすまない. Indicates that society or conscience demands the action.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
約束を破ったのだから、説明し{___}。
(Having broken a promise, you cannot get away without explaining.)
explanation: ないではすまない (must inevitably) — 説明しないではすまない. The broken promise creates a social obligation to explain.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
人に怪我をさせたのだから、責任を取ら{___}。
(Having injured someone, you cannot get away without taking responsibility.)
explanation: ないではすまない (must inevitably) — 責任を取らないではすまない. Moral and social accountability.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この映画は見た人を感動させ{___}。
(This film will definitely move everyone who sees it.)
explanation: ないではおかない (will definitely/inevitably) — 感動させないではおかない. Something has such power that it will inevitably produce the effect.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼の演技は観客を笑わせ{___}。
(His acting will definitely make the audience laugh.)
explanation: ないではおかない (will definitely) — 笑わせないではおかない. The subject's power to produce a reaction is presented as irresistible.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
その音楽は聴く人を涙ぐませ{___}。
(That music will definitely bring tears to those who hear it.)
explanation: ないではおかない (will inevitably) — 涙ぐませないではおかない. Describes an emotionally irresistible piece of music.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この本はあなたの人生観を変え{___}。
(This book will definitely change your outlook on life.)
explanation: ないではおかない (will definitely) — 変えないではおかない. The book's power to transform the reader is presented as certain.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
これほど貢献した人には、もっと評価され{___}。
(Someone who has contributed this much should be more highly appreciated.)
explanation: てしかるべきだ (should rightly be/it is appropriate that) — もっと評価されてしかるべきだ. Asserts that the outcome is only right and proper.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
こんな重大な問題は、議会で議論され{___}。
(Such a serious issue should rightly be debated in parliament.)
explanation: てしかるべきだ (should rightly be) — 議会で議論されてしかるべきだ. Formal assertion of what proper procedure demands.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼のような才能は、もっと早く認められ{___}。
(Talent like his should rightly have been recognised much sooner.)
explanation: てしかるべきだ (should rightly be) — 認められてしかるべきだ. Expresses regret that something proper was not done.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
これだけの実績があれば、昇進し{___}。
(With a track record like this, being promoted would only be right.)
explanation: てしかるべきだ (it is only right that) — 昇進してしかるべきだ. Argues that the promotion is warranted by the evidence.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼女の幸せを願っ{___}。
(I ceaselessly wish for her happiness.)
explanation: てやまない (ceaselessly/from the bottom of one's heart) — 彼女の幸せを願ってやまない. Formal; expresses an enduring and deep wish or feeling.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この国の発展を期待し{___}。
(I ceaselessly expect the development of this country.)
explanation: てやまない (ceaselessly) — この国の発展を期待してやまない. Formal; used in speeches to express enduring hope.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
皆さんのご健康を祈っ{___}。
(I ceaselessly pray for everyone's good health.)
explanation: てやまない (ceaselessly/from the bottom of one's heart) — 皆さんのご健康を祈ってやまない. Formal closing expression in speeches.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
亡き師の教えを尊敬し{___}。
(I ceaselessly respect the teachings of my late teacher.)
explanation: てやまない (ceaselessly) — 亡き師の教えを尊敬してやまない. Formal expression of lasting veneration.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
頑張った{___}、こんな素晴らしい結果になった。
(Because there was worth in working hard, such a wonderful result came about.)
explanation: 甲斐がある (worth doing/it pays off) — 頑張った甲斐がある. Confirms that effort produced meaningful reward.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
教えた{___}、彼はどんどん上手くなっている。
(There is worth in teaching him — he is getting better and better.)
explanation: 甲斐がある (worth it) — 教えた甲斐がある. The student's progress justifies the teacher's effort.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
毎日練習した{___}、試合に勝てた。
(There was worth in practising every day — I was able to win the match.)
explanation: 甲斐がある (worth it/paying off) — 毎日練習した甲斐がある。Success vindicates the daily effort.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
苦労し{___}、こんなに成長した。
(There was worth in suffering hardship — look how much I have grown.)
explanation: 甲斐がある (worth it) — 苦労した甲斐がある。Hardship is reframed as investment with visible returns.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
必死の看護の{___}、患者は亡くなった。
(Despite desperate nursing, to no avail, the patient passed away.)
explanation: 甲斐もなく (to no avail/despite one's efforts) — 必死の看護の甲斐もなく、患者は亡くなった. Expresses grief that effort was ultimately futile.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
説得を試みた{___}、彼は聞き入れなかった。
(Despite attempting persuasion, to no avail, he did not listen.)
explanation: 甲斐もなく (to no avail) — 説得を試みた甲斐もなく、彼は聞き入れなかった. Effort was expended but produced no result.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
全力を尽くした{___}、試合に負けてしまった。
(Despite giving everything, to no avail, we lost the match.)
explanation: 甲斐もなく (to no avail) — 全力を尽くした甲斐もなく、試合に負けてしまった. Disappointment when maximum effort still fails.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
長年の研究の{___}、成果は出なかった。
(Despite years of research, to no avail, no results emerged.)
explanation: 甲斐もなく (to no avail) — 長年の研究の甲斐もなく、成果は出なかった. Long-term investment yielding nothing.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
こんなに人を思いやる行動が愛情{___}。
(If such a considerate action is not love, then what is it?)
explanation: でなくてなんだろう (what else could it be but) — 愛情でなくてなんだろう. Rhetorical question asserting that the label is the only possible one.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
命を賭けて人を救う行為が英雄{___}。
(If risking one's life to save a person is not heroism, what is it?)
explanation: でなくてなんだろう (what else but) — 英雄でなくてなんだろう. Rhetorical force asserts an obvious conclusion.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
これほどの苦しみが試練{___}。
(If suffering this much is not a trial, what is it?)
explanation: でなくてなんだろう (what else but) — 試練でなくてなんだろう. Reframes unbearable suffering as purposeful challenge.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
40年間同じ店を守り続けたことが情熱{___}。
(If protecting the same shop for 40 years is not passion, what is it?)
explanation: でなくてなんだろう (what else but) — 情熱でなくてなんだろう. Long dedication reframed as undeniable proof of passion.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
これこそが真の勇気という者{___}。
(Could this not be what is truly called courage?)
explanation: ではあるまいか (isn't it/could it not be — rhetorical, formal) — 真の勇気という者ではあるまいか. Formal rhetorical question leading the reader to a conclusion.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この問題こそが、現代社会が直面すべき最大の課題{___}。
(Could this problem not be the greatest challenge modern society must face?)
explanation: ではあるまいか (isn't it) — 最大の課題ではあるまいか. Formal rhetorical assertion common in essays and speeches.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
これこそが人類に残された最後のチャンス{___}。
(Could this not be humanity's last remaining chance?)
explanation: ではあるまいか (could it not be) — 最後のチャンスではあるまいか. Urgent rhetorical question in formal writing.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
今こそ行動すべき時{___}。
(Could this not be the time when we should act?)
explanation: ではあるまいか (isn't it) — 行動すべき時ではあるまいか. Formal call to action framed as rhetorical question.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
起こる{___}起きた事故だ。
(It was an accident that happened as it was destined to happen.)
explanation: べくして (as destined to/naturally) — 起こるべくして起きた事故だ. The event was predictable; an inevitable outcome of the conditions.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
勝つ{___}勝ったチームだ。
(It is a team that won as it was destined to win.)
explanation: べくして (as destined to) — 勝つべくして勝ったチームだ. The victory was the natural result of superior preparation.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
失敗する{___}失敗した計画だった。
(It was a plan that failed as it was destined to fail.)
explanation: べくして (as destined to) — 失敗するべくして失敗した計画だった. Hindsight shows the failure was built in from the start.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
出会う{___}出会った二人だと思う。
(I think they are two people who met as they were destined to meet.)
explanation: べくして (as destined to) — 出会うべくして出会った二人だ. Romantic framing of fate.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
待ってましたっ{___}、彼は手を挙げた。
(As if to say 'I've been waiting for this!', he raised his hand.)
explanation: とばかりに (as if to say) — 待ってましたっとばかりに、彼は手を挙げた. Describes a vivid action that communicates a message without words.
